raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Over 20% of California's energy generation comes from solar.",
    "Approximately 45% of households in California are renters who cannot install a solar system.",
    "AB 2316 establishes a statewide community solar and storage program in California.",
    "AB 2316 represents the most significant expansion of solar access to low- and middle-income communities in California's history.",
    "Customers of privately-owned utilities can sign up or decline to participate in the community solar storage program.",
    "Customers who participate in the community solar storage program will receive a credit on their utility bill for their share of power produced.",
    "Financial incentives will be available for low-income customers participating in the community solar storage program.",
    "Subscribers to the community solar program are compensated based on the value of the renewable energy resource determined by the privately-owned utility.",
    "AB 2316 will increase the reliability of solar energy by ensuring clean energy is sent to the electrical grid at peak hours.",
    "AB 2316 will offer solar access to homeowners or business owners who cannot afford to install their own photovoltaic systems or face structural challenges."
  ]
}
```
